tasks_completed.description: Replaced bare task-id grep with machine-readable marker grep at commands/commit.md:157 and updated prose label
tasks_completed.changes: Line 157: grep -lF "${TASK_ID}" candidate.md -> grep -lF "<!-- spec-continuation-of: ${TASK_ID} -->" candidate.md; 'literal-task-id grep' -> 'machine-readable marker grep'
tasks_completed.rationale: Root cause: bare grep matched any prose occurrence of task-id (including cross-references and historical notes), causing false-positive stage-3 candidates. The marker-based predicate requires explicit machine-readable declaration of continuation intent.

git_rationale.root_cause_commit: 1a1d058 feat(dev-cycle): Cycle 2 remediation — the bare grep was introduced as AC5 fix in cycle 20260519-211515 with false-positive risk documented as known limitation
git_rationale.why_issue_occurred: The literal task-id grep had zero semantic discrimination between 'this spec IS the continuation spec' and 'this spec MENTIONS the task-id in prose'. Specs that cite prior cycle task-ids in cross-references (3 matches in spec-20260518-225715.md) or retrospective analysis (7 matches in spec-20260520-221059.md) all became false-positive candidates.
git_rationale.how_fix_addresses_root: The marker grep only matches the HTML comment <!-- spec-continuation-of: <task-id> --> which can only appear via explicit authorial intent. Prose references, cross-references, and historical notes never contain this marker, so they are excluded from the candidate set entirely.

dev.qa_notes: All ACs verified statically. AC1/AC4: grep -lF '<!-- spec-continuation-of: 20260519-211515 -->' docs/dev/specs/spec-20260518-225715.md docs/dev/specs/spec-20260520-221059.md returns exit 1 (no matches). AC3: 'multiple specs produced this cycle without context linkage' still at line 162. AC3b: 'spec produced this cycle but not linked in context' still at line 161. AC5: 'literal-task-id grep' removed; 'machine-readable marker grep' present at line 157. The stage-3 header on line 156 ('Mtime + literal-task-id glob') was intentionally left unchanged — BA spec scoped the edit to line 157 body only.
implementation_notes: The section header at line 156 still reads '(3) Mtime + literal-task-id glob (final stage).' — BA spec explicitly restricted changes to line 157 only. If QA flags the header as stale, a separate ticket is needed.

AC2 (marker-bearing spec IS a candidate) cannot be verified statically against existing spec files since no spec in docs/dev/specs/ currently contains a marker. The grep pattern itself is logically correct; AC2 is exercisable once any future spec ships the marker.

self_verification.notes: Static grep checks: (1) new grep pattern confirmed at line 157; (2) old 'literal-task-id grep' prose absent; (3) 'machine-readable marker grep' present at line 157; (4) fail-closed branches at lines 161 and 162 unchanged; (5) marker grep against two known false-positive spec files returns exit 1 as expected

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Two OBSERVATION_ONLY findings — zero blockers. (1) No requirement-blocking issue found; commands/commit.md:157 now uses the exact marker grep, removes the bare predicate, and preserves both fail-closed branches. (2) commands/commit.md:156 still says 'Mtime + literal-task-id glob (final stage)' — stale wording but not a blocker since scope was restricted to line 157 only by BA spec.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: No changes required. Both findings are OBSERVATION_ONLY with no PROPOSED_FIX. Finding 2 (stale header on line 156) is already documented in implementation_notes and recommendations; it is out-of-scope per BA spec.

codex_observation_only: commands/commit.md:156 still reads '(3) Mtime + literal-task-id glob (final stage).' — slightly stale wording relative to the new predicate. Not a blocker; BA spec scoped changes to line 157 only.
recommendations: Update the stage-3 section header at line 156 from 'Mtime + literal-task-id glob' to 'Mtime + marker-grep' in a follow-on ticket to keep prose consistent with the new predicate.
BA/orchestrator should add marker-placement instructions to the spec-creation workflow so future continuation specs ship with <!-- spec-continuation-of: <task-id> --> at the top.
